anzsic_codes.name: Management Advice and Related Consulting Services

merger_description:
Seismic Software, Inc (**Seismic**) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital of Highspot, Inc. (**Highspot**), via merger.

Both Seismic and Highspot are US-headquartered businesses which offer sales enablement software platforms and products to Australian customers as well as globally. Sales enablement software helps improve enterprise sales teams’ performance by providing content management and collaboration capabilities, data analytics capabilities and learning and coaching modules to build sales skills. Both parties’ sales enablement platforms are enhanced by artificial intelligence. Both parties supply their products to customers across a range of industry sectors.

Seismic is majority owned by Permira Advisers LLP (**Permira**). Permira would remain the controlling shareholder of Seismic following the proposed acquisition.

events.title: Seismic - Highspot - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Seismic Software, Inc (Seismic) proposes to acquire 100% of the
share capital of Highspot, Inc. (Highspot) by way of merger (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

Both the acquirer, Seismic, and the target, Highspot, are US-
headquartered businesses which offer sales enablement software
platforms and products to Australian customers as well as globally.
Sales enablement software helps improve enterprise sales teams’
performance by providing content management and collaboration
capabilities, data analytics capabilities and learning and coaching
modules to build sales skills. Both parties’ sales enablement
platforms are enhanced by artificial intelligence. Both parties supply
their products to customers across a range of industry sectors.
Seismic is majority owned by Permira Advisers LLP (Permira).
Permira would remain the controlling shareholder of Seismic
following the Acquisition.

The parties overlap in the supply of sales enablement software
globally. The parties supply integrated sales enablement platforms
that offer a full range of sales enablement applications and also
offer each sales enablement application separately.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third parties and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market in
Australia. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before
it, the ACCC makes the following findings.
• The parties both have a limited presence in Australia, with few
customers and relatively low revenue.
• Most Australian customers of sales enablement software
acquire one or more single applications from the overall range of
sales enablement applications, rather than a full platform
solution. Information before the ACCC indicates that there are
multiple alternative suppliers of the range of single applications
offered by the parties. While some Australian customers use a
full platform solution for which there are fewer alternatives, they
did not express concerns about the impact of the transaction on
competition.
• The ACCC closely considered information, including third party
feedback, regarding Seismic’s strong position in the supply of
sales enablement software platforms and the potential for the
Acquisition to further strengthen that position. However, given
that alternatives, such as Showpad, will remain available, and
taking into account other information including third party
feedback, the ACCC found that customers would continue to
have a range of competitive options for sales enablement
software post-acquisition.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
